commands: conda run -n ptarcade python -c '<compute m_W, m_chi, beta_lambda over PTA 1-sigma region>'
WebSearch: supercooled FOPT reheating temperature BBN dark sector NANOGrav MeV
WebSearch: classically conformal dark vector DM relic NANOGrav SU(2) Neff
WebSearch: lower bound reheating temperature BBN MeV neutrino thermalization 4.7 MeV
WebSearch: kinetic mixing dark photon constraints 100-600 MeV supernova beam dump
warnings: The model as specified is SECLUDED: no tree-level portal (kinetic mixing or Higgs portal) between the dark SU(2)_D sector and the SM is given. Yet handoff_model.json assumes thermal equilibrium with the SM (xi=1). This is internally inconsistent: equilibrium requires a portal, and a portal generically breaks classical conformal invariance (Higgs portal) or adds U(1) (kinetic mixing requires an abelian factor, absent in pure SU(2)_D). This unresolved portal question controls almost every cosmological constraint below.

Several binding constraints (BBN reheating, Neff/dark radiation, relic abundance) cannot be evaluated to a definitive pass/fail without computing the dark-sector energy budget after percolation and the rate of energy transfer to the SM, which are NOT in any upstream handoff.

Treh in handoff_fopt.json benchmarks (~0.5-1.5 MeV) and pta T_star (~37-138 MeV) use the spectrum 'reheating-temperature' convention; whether the SM photon-neutrino bath actually reaches >~2-4.7 MeV depends on energy transfer and is not established.
approximations: Derived masses m_W=g*chi0/2 and m_chi=sqrt(beta_lambda)*chi0 evaluated at PTArcade 1-sigma corners and MAP; beta_lambda=9*(g/2)^4/(8 pi^2) with negligible scalar self-loop (<0.04% per critic).

BBN reheating lower bound taken as Treh >~ 2-4.7 MeV (oscillation-corrected to ~2 MeV; Planck-era ~4.7 MeV) from low-reheating literature, applied to the SM bath temperature, not the dark-sector temperature.

m_W_GeV.note: 3 degenerate dark gauge bosons, vector DM candidate

m_chi_GeV.note: CW pseudo-dilaton; m_chi < m_W < 2*m_W so chi->W W kinematically closed, chi is long-lived in the secluded sector

notes: MAP m_chi (71 MeV) < m_W (486 MeV): the pseudo-dilaton cannot decay to on-shell dark gauge bosons; with no SM portal it has no decay channel and is cosmologically stable/long-lived -> potential late-decaying or dark-radiation/relic problem.
m_chi ~ 37-91 MeV and m_W ~ 0.26-0.59 GeV are all sub-GeV, squarely in the range probed by beam-dump, supernova and CMB/BBN energy-injection constraints IF a SM portal exists.

Treh (spectrum convention) at MAP ~ 0.1 GeV from pta benchmarks; fopt benchmarks at deeper supercooling give Treh down to ~0.5-1.5 MeV. The relevant BBN quantity is the post-transition SM bath temperature.

constraints.name: BBN lower bound on reheating temperature
constraints.description: A supercooled FOPT dumps the vacuum energy into the plasma; the SM photon-neutrino bath must reheat above ~2-4.7 MeV so that neutrinos re-thermalize and light-element abundances are preserved. If the latent heat stays in the dark sector (no portal) or the SM bath is reheated below this, BBN is spoiled.
missing_observables: SM-only bath temperature after percolation and entropy transfer

conventions.Treh_handoff: spectrum reheating-temperature convention (combined bath), not necessarily SM photon-neutrino bath

constraints.applicability: Binding in principle over the entire PTA-preferred region; the fopt benchmarks reach Treh ~0.5-1.5 MeV (deep supercooling) which would violate the >~2 MeV bound, while pta T_star ~37-138 MeV at the PTA-preferred chi0~1 GeV would satisfy it IF that energy reaches the SM bath.

tested_subregion.note: PTA 1-sigma region has T_star/Treh ~0.04-0.14 GeV (above 2 MeV); deeper-supercooling fopt benchmarks at chi0~few MeV fall below 2 MeV but are outside the PTA 1-sigma region.

applicability_notes: Bai/Korwar-type cosmological-FOPT bound and low-reheating literature: Treh >~ 4.7 MeV (Planck) relaxing to ~2 MeV with neutrino oscillations.
Cannot be declared pass/fail until SM-bath reheating is computed; the secluded model gives NO SM energy transfer, in which case the SM bath is NOT reheated and the bound is effectively violated.

source.title: Cosmological constraints on first-order phase transitions; Bounds on very low reheating scenarios after Planck
source.authors: Y. Bai, M. Korwar; P. F. de Salas et al.

constraints.name: Dark radiation / Neff from a secluded dark sector
constraints.description: If the dark SU(2)_D sector does not transfer its energy to the SM before neutrino decoupling, its relativistic content (gauge bosons, pseudo-dilaton, dark thermal bath) contributes dark radiation Delta Neff, constrained by Planck/BBN.
required_inputs: dark-to-SM temperature ratio xi at neutrino decoupling

conventions.Planck: Neff = 2.99 +/- 0.17; Delta Neff > 0.22 excluded at 95% CL

constraints.applicability: Binding over the whole PTA-preferred region IF the sector stays secluded. NANOGrav-dedicated study 2306.09411 states a completely secluded dark-sector FOPT is strongly disfavoured.

tested_subregion.note: Entire PTA 1-sigma region, conditional on no portal.

applicability_notes: 2306.09411: 'a phase transition in a completely secluded dark sector is strongly disfavoured'; Delta Neff > 0.22 excluded (95% CL).

The model assumes xi=1 (full equilibrium) which REQUIRES a portal; with a portal that decays the dark sector to the SM before nu decoupling, Delta Neff can be acceptable. Quantitative xi and Delta Neff are not computed anywhere upstream.

source.title: Does NANOGrav observe a dark sector phase transition?
source.authors: Bringmann, Depta, Hufnagel, Kersten, Mariotti, et al.

constraints.name: Portal requirement / thermalization with SM bath
constraints.description: handoff_model.json assumes dark-SM thermal equilibrium (xi=1) but specifies NO portal. Maintaining equilibrium and draining the dark-sector energy to the SM both require a portal. In pure SU(2)_D (no U(1)) kinetic mixing is forbidden at renormalizable level; a Higgs portal |Phi|^2|H|^2 breaks the classical conformal symmetry that the whole CW mechanism relies on.
constraints.observable: thermalization rate vs Hubble; conformal-symmetry breaking from portal
constraints.applicability: Foundational: determines whether C1/C2/C5/C7-C10 are evaluated in the equilibrium or secluded regime. The model is currently under-specified on this point.
applicability_notes: Sister model 2502.19478 (conformal U(1)' has a dark photon) uses kinetic mixing kappa ~ 1e-7..1e-3 to keep equilibrium and decay the sector to e+e-; that mechanism is unavailable in pure non-abelian SU(2)_D without adding fields.
Resolving the portal is a prerequisite to closing C1 and C2; flag to model/proposer/critic.

source.title: Sub-GeV dark matter and nano-Hertz GW from a classically conformal dark sector

constraints.name: Transition completion / percolation in deep supercooling
constraints.description: Strongly supercooled (alpha>>1) conformal transitions risk failing to percolate or leading to eternal false-vacuum/inflation. Already checked by fopt-agent for benchmarks, but the PTA-preferred region must percolate without runaway.
constraints.observable: percolation temperature Tp finite; eternal_inflation False
constraints.applicability: Satisfied: fopt-agent verified percolation (Tp finite) and eternal_inflation=False for all viable points; pta-agent confirmed viability across the 1-sigma window.
applicability_notes: This is a self-consistency/theory check, already passed upstream; recorded for completeness.

source.title: Supercooled phase transitions in conformal dark sectors explain NANOGrav data

constraints.name: Vector dark matter relic abundance
constraints.description: The 3 degenerate dark gauge bosons W_D (m~0.26-0.59 GeV) are a vector DM candidate. Their relic abundance must not overclose the Universe (Omega h^2 <= 0.12). In the secluded model the abundance is set by dark-sector freeze-out and/or non-thermal production via the (unspecified) portal.
required_inputs: W_D annihilation cross section (W W -> chi chi etc.)
constraints.applicability: Applies across the region if W_D is the DM. Whether under-, over-, or correctly abundant cannot be stated without a relic calculation. In 2109.11558 (same model) the relic is set non-thermally via scalar-portal mixing.

tested_subregion.note: Whole region; not evaluable without cross-section/portal.

applicability_notes: Custodial SO(4) of the single doublet can render the W_D triplet stable (vector DM); confirm stability.
2109.11558 obtains the relic non-thermally via scalar-portal mixing; requires the same portal that C3 flags as unspecified.

source.title: A first order dark SU(2)_D phase transition with vector dark matter (NANOGrav 12.5yr)

constraints.name: Pseudo-dilaton (chi) cosmological stability / late decays
constraints.description: At the PTA-preferred point m_chi (37-91 MeV) < m_W (0.26-0.59 GeV), so chi->W W is closed. In a strictly secluded sector chi has no decay channel and is cosmologically stable, acting as extra (cold or warm) matter/dark radiation; with a portal it decays to SM (e+e-, gamma gamma) and is constrained by BBN/CMB energy injection depending on its lifetime.
constraints.observable: chi lifetime and abundance; BBN/CMB energy injection
constraints.applicability: Binding in the secluded case (stable light scalar => overproduction / dark radiation) and in the portal case (late e+e-/gamma injection constrained by BBN and CMB). Cannot be evaluated without lifetime and abundance.

tested_subregion.note: Whole region; m_chi always < m_W so chi->WW closed throughout.

applicability_notes: If chi decays after ~0.1 s with e+e- or photons, BBN/CMB energy-injection bounds apply (cf. 2502.19478 tau_phi ~ 2500 s flagged as constrained).
If chi is stable and relic, it adds dark matter/dark radiation -> Neff or Omega bounds.

source.title: Sub-GeV dark matter and nano-Hertz GW from a classically conformal dark sector

constraints.name: Beam-dump / fixed-target bounds on a kinetic-mixing portal
constraints.description: IF the required SM portal is kinetic mixing epsilon of a dark vector with mass ~0.26-0.59 GeV, beam-dump experiments (E137, CHARM, nu-cal) exclude epsilon ~ 1e-7..1e-4 for 1 MeV-1 GeV masses, overlapping the kappa ~ 1e-6..1e-4 window used by the sister model to drain the dark sector.
constraints.observable: visible dark-photon decay yield in beam dump
missing_observables: epsilon (model has no abelian factor for kinetic mixing)
constraints.applicability: Conditional: only if a kinetic-mixing portal is added (not possible for pure non-abelian SU(2)_D at renormalizable level). If a U(1) is introduced to host the portal, this beam-dump band directly tensions the epsilon window needed for BBN/relic.

tested_subregion.note: m_W~0.26-0.59 GeV overlaps E137/CHARM/nu-cal coverage; epsilon needed for thermal contact (~1e-6..1e-4) overlaps excluded band.

applicability_notes: Do NOT apply unless the portal and epsilon are specified; current model has neither.  Recast required against the specific W_D decay structure.

source.title: Constraints on kinetic mixing of dark photons from dilepton spectra; beam-dump compilations

constraints.name: Supernova (SN1987A) cooling bound on a light portal mediator
constraints.description: IF a kinetic-mixing portal exists, a dark vector with mass <~ O(100 MeV) and epsilon ~ 1e-10..1e-6 can be emitted from the SN1987A core and cause excess cooling. The PTA-preferred m_W~0.26-0.59 GeV is at/above the upper edge of the strongest SN exclusion; m_chi~37-91 MeV is in the SN-sensitive window if it couples to SM.
constraints.applicability: Conditional on a portal. The SN1987A bound bites mainly at lighter masses / smaller epsilon than the thermal-contact window, so it likely constrains a different slice than C7; record subregion, do not exclude full region.

tested_subregion.note: Relevant primarily for m_chi~37-91 MeV and epsilon~1e-9..1e-6 IF chi/W_D couple to SM.

applicability_notes: Not applicable to the strictly secluded model. Recast needed once a portal is defined.

source.title: Supernova bounds on the dark photon using its electromagnetic decay

constraints.name: Direct detection of sub-GeV vector dark matter
constraints.description: If W_D is DM and couples to the SM via the (unspecified) portal, sub-GeV DM-electron/nucleon scattering bounds (e.g. low-threshold experiments) could apply. For a strictly secluded sector with no portal there is no DM-SM scattering and direct detection does not apply.
constraints.applicability: Not applicable in the secluded model (no DM-SM coupling). Becomes recast_needed only if a portal large enough for scattering is introduced; sub-GeV DM generically evades current heavy-WIMP direct-detection limits (cf. 2502.19478).
applicability_notes: Listed for completeness; flips to recast_needed only with a portal.

source.title: Sub-GeV dark matter and nano-Hertz GW from a classically conformal dark sector

constraints.name: DM self-interaction (SIDM) bound on light vector DM
constraints.description: Light vector DM with a light mediator (here chi, m_chi~37-91 MeV, lighter than m_W) can have large self-interactions; the Bullet-cluster / halo-shape bound sigma/m <~ 1 cm^2/g constrains the gauge coupling g and mass spectrum.
constraints.observable: DM self-scattering cross section per mass sigma/m
constraints.applicability: Applies if W_D is the DM; sub-GeV DM with O(1) gauge coupling and a ~10x lighter mediator can be in the strongly self-interacting regime. Evaluable with a self-interaction calculation; all required masses/couplings ARE available.
applicability_notes: Required inputs are all present in handoffs; only the cross-section computation is missing (medium priority).

constraints.name: Collider invisible/visible width (Higgs portal) bounds
constraints.description: IF the SM portal were the Higgs portal |Phi|^2|H|^2, it would induce Higgs invisible/exotic decays and contribute to SM Higgs signal strength; LHC invisible-Higgs limits would then apply. The Higgs portal also breaks classical conformal invariance, undermining the CW mechanism.
constraints.applicability: Conditional on a Higgs portal, which conflicts with conformal invariance; not applicable to the secluded conformal model as written. Recorded so the portal trade-off is explicit.
applicability_notes: Adding a Higgs portal would break the CW symmetry the model is built on; flag tension to proposer/critic.

missing_calculations.observable: SM-bath reheating temperature after percolation and dark-to-SM energy transfer
required_inputs: gstar in dark and SM sectors
missing_calculations.suggested_backend_module: reheating_and_energy_transfer (new); could extend semianalytic_pipeline outputs (alpha, Treh, gstar) with an entropy-transfer step
validation_checks: energy conservation between vacuum energy, dark bath and SM bath

missing_calculations.observable: Dark-to-SM temperature ratio xi(T) and Delta Neff at BBN/CMB
Portal operator and coupling (thermalization rate vs Hubble; conformal-breaking check)
required_inputs: model extension: U(1) for kinetic mixing or |Phi|^2|H|^2 Higgs portal
missing_calculations.suggested_backend_module: model-level decision (proposer/critic), then thermalization rate module
validation_checks: Gamma_portal/H >= 1 at relevant T for equilibrium
portal does not reintroduce an explicit mass scale that spoils CW

missing_calculations.observable: W_D vector-DM relic abundance and W_D lifetime/stability
validation_checks: W_D lifetime > age of Universe if it is the DM

missing_calculations.observable: chi (pseudo-dilaton) decay width, lifetime and relic abundance
required_inputs: available SM final states (e+e-, gamma gamma)
validation_checks: tau_chi vs BBN (t<~0.1 s) and CMB energy-injection windows

missing_calculations.observable: W_D self-interaction cross section per mass sigma/m
